steps.title: Use the counterfactual limit
steps.content: The passage says it may be tempting to ask how a handwritten novel would differ if it had been typed instead, but each novel results from a unique set of circumstances. That uniqueness prevents a reasonable comparison with an alternate version that never existed.

steps.title: Reject unsupported conclusions
steps.content: Choice A recommends changing authors' methods. Choice B claims handwriting leads to more success. Choice C makes an unsupported claim about production time. None follows from the point about each novel's unique circumstances. The answer is D.
